news.headline: Hyundai Motor Group launches Pleos software brand at Pleos 25 developer conference, unveiling new SDV technologies and collaborations.
news.summary: At the recent Pleos 25 developer conference, The Group introduced its software brand, Pleos, and detailed its strategy for the software-driven 'Cloud Mobility' ecosystem. They revealed a proprietary vehicle operating system aimed at enhancing self-driving vehicle (SDV) efficiency and scalability through an innovative Electrical and Electronic (E&E) architecture. The next phase, Pleos Connect, promises to deliver a cutting-edge solution for the future.

news.headline: HashKey Group and Bosera have launched the world's first Tokenised Money Market ETF, merging traditional finance with blockchain.
news.summary: A groundbreaking Money Market ETF has been introduced to revolutionize the convergence of conventional finance and blockchain technology. HashKey Group ("HashKey"), a prominent digital asset financial services group in Asia, in partnership with Bosera Asset Management, launched this innovative ETF in Hong Kong on March 28, 2025. This partnership aims to combine traditional financial practices with cutting-edge blockchain technology, marking a significant step in the financial sector's modernization.

Shiba Inu attracts more long-term holders than Bitcoin and Ethereum, with 76% holding for over a year compared to 74% for BTC and ETH. Whale concentration raises concerns.
news.summary: Long-term holders of Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) now hold more of its supply than Bitcoin and Ethereum. Currently, 76% of SHIB holders have kept their tokens for over a year, with 22% holding for over a month but less than a year, and only 2% holding for less than a month. In contrast, approximately 74% of Bitcoin and Ethereum investors have held onto their tokens for over a year. It is worth noting that Bitcoin and Ethereum have larger market capitalizations. Detailed information can be found on Benzinga.com.

Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises set to release Q4 results; expected loss at 5 cents/share, revenue at $213.07M.
news.summary: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (NYSE:BW) is set to announce its fourth-quarter financial results on March 31 after the market closes. Analysts predict a quarterly loss of 5 cents per share, an improvement from the 18 cents per share loss reported last year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue to be around $213.07 million compared to $227.2 million in the same period last year, based on data from Benzinga Pro. In February, Babcock & Wilcox's construction subsidiary secured contracts worth $35 million for maintenance and service tasks at power plants. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: South Carolina drops lawsuit against Coinbase over staking, joining Vermont, $2 million in lost staking rewards, Bitcoin reserve bill introduced. United with Vermont, South Carolina dismisses unfounded staking lawsuit against Coinbase, with South Carolina residents losing an estimated $2 million in staking rewards. $2 million lost in staking rewards, South Carolina introduces Bitcoin reserve bill, federal government also looks to establish a high ceiling Bitcoin reserve.
news.summary: South Carolina has dropped its lawsuit against crypto exchange Coinbase for allegedly offering unregistered securities through its staking services. The lawsuit was dismissed on March 27, following a joint stipulation between Coinbase and the South Carolina Attorney General's securities division. South Carolina and Vermont were among 10 states that filed legal actions against Coinbase on June 6, 2023. The lawsuit caused South Carolina residents to lose an estimated $2 million in staking rewards. Meanwhile, South Carolina introduced the "Strategic Digital Assets Reserve Act" which allows the state treasurer to allocate up to 10% of certain state funds to cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. The bill mentions establishing a Bitcoin reserve of up to 1 million Bitcoin, without limiting other digital assets. Additionally, the US federal government also aims to reach or exceed a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. This move reflects an increasing interest in adopting cryptocurrencies at the state level in the US.

Cryptsoft's SDKs now support post-quantum cryptography algorithms, enhancing existing products.
news.summary: In Brisbane, Australia, as of March 28, 2025, Cryptsoft has announced the addition of support for interoperable post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms in their KMIP and PKCS#11 SDKs. These updated SDKs build upon the company's already successful offerings and have been verified for enhanced security. This update marks a significant advancement in the field of encryption technology, providing users with cutting-edge solutions to address modern cybersecurity threats.

Over 100K Gemini, Binance users' data for sale by darkweb actors, including names, passwords, and locations. Source claims data not from exchange leak..BufferedReader: Darkweb threat actors claim to have hundreds of thousands of user records — names, passwords and location data — of Gemini and Binance users, for sale on the internet. Source: Dark Web Informer.
news.summary: Threat actors on the dark web are claiming to have user records from Gemini and Binance users on sale, including names, passwords, and location data. The Dark Web Informer reported that AKM69 is selling a database containing 100,000 records of individuals mainly from the US, with some from Singapore and the UK. Another user named kiki88888 was offering to sell Binance emails and passwords, with 132,744 lines of information compromised. Binance clarified that the leaked data was collected through compromised browser sessions on infected computers, not from a data leak. Last September, a hacker claimed to have stolen 12.8 million records from Binance, but the exchange denied the claims after an internal investigation. There have been recent cyber threats targeting users of major crypto exchanges, including scams spoofing sender IDs of legitimate exchanges like Binance and phishing attempts on users of Coinbase and Gemini.

news.headline: India is attracting American businesses with ample skilled young professionals despite Trump's tariffs. Hiring spree underway.
news.summary: The presence of a substantial number of enthusiastic young professionals is attracting American businesses to establish their international operations in Indian urban centers.

news.headline: Australia faces close election; US aims to influence Ukraine's economy. Read more in the Bloomberg Asia Trade on 3/28/25.
news.summary: Australia is experiencing a closely contested election as vote counting is ongoing, with both major parties vying for control. The outcome is uncertain as the margin is narrow, with each side holding approximately 48-49% of votes. On the international front, the US is looking to assert influence over Ukraine's economy, with aims to exercise control over its financial and trade policies. These developments were covered in Bloomberg's Asia Trade report dated 3/28/25.

news.headline: Keystone Dental names Tony Susino CEO & Board Member in Irvine, Calif. on March 28, 2025.
news.summary: Keystone Dental Holdings, Inc., headquartered in Irvine, California, made a significant announcement on March 28, 2025. The company disclosed the appointment of Tony Susino as the new Chief Executive Officer and member of its Board of Directors. Susino will officially assume his roles without delay. With a distinguished background and a history of achieving strategic objectives and driving commercial prosperity, Susino's leadership is expected to steer Keystone Dental Holdings towards further success.

GameStop's short sales spiked 234%, triggering NYSE's Short Sale Restriction as stock fell 22% to $22.09. Bitcoin purchase raises doubts.
news.summary: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) imposed a Short Sale Restriction (SSR) on GameStop after short sales volume spiked to 30.85 million shares on March 27, a 234% increase in 24 hours. GameStop's stock fell 22%, triggering SSR as it dropped over 10% from the previous day. Some analysts question GameStop's plan to purchase Bitcoin after announcing a $1.3 billion convertible notes offering. The stock decline may be linked to the offering as seen with a similar case involving MicroStrategy in 2021. The biggest day of short sales remains at 46.20 million on June 3, 2024, coinciding with trader Keith Gill’s return to GameStop stock. GameStop said the notes offering will be used for general corporate purposes, including acquiring Bitcoin. Some analysts see potential for a short squeeze if GameStop or Bitcoin prices surge. This article does not provide investment advice.

Over $12B Bitcoin options to expire, causing market volatility. $27.98B total BTC options, 43% expiring. Put/call ratio 0.49, suggesting bullish sentiment.
news.summary: Investors are anticipating a significant event in the cryptocurrency market with the impending expiry of over $12 billion worth of Bitcoin options on Friday. Deribit, a prominent cryptocurrency derivatives exchange, has reported that at 4:00 a.m. ET, Bitcoin options contracts valued at approximately $12.14 billion will expire. This expiry represents 43% of the total Open Interest for BTC options, which is valued at about $27.98 billion. Options are financial instruments that grant the buyer the right, though not the obligation, to buy or sell the underlying asset at a set price and date. A call option allows for the purchase of an asset, while a put option enables the sale of assets at a predetermined price. Currently, the put/call ratio stands at 0.49, indicating a bullish sentiment as more traders are placing their bets on price movements. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

PPI inflation flat, S&P 500 up, airfares weigh down rally. Core prices steady, CPI data awaited amidst Trump tariffs.
news.summary: The latest data on the producer price index revealed that core prices remained unchanged last month. This development contributed to a rise in the S&P 500 prior to the upcoming release of the Consumer Price Index tomorrow, despite ongoing concerns surrounding Trump's tariff announcements. The subdued inflation indicated by the PPI numbers had a positive impact on the market, with the S&P 500 showing gains. However, this rally was dampened by a decrease in airfares. The market anticipates further insights with the impending CPI announcement.

news.headline: Fed chair Powell warns of policy uncertainty under Trump affecting Fed rate outlook. Read on Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: Jerome Powell, the Federal Reserve Chairman, has expressed concerns about the impact of "policy uncertainty" during Donald Trump's administration on the Federal Reserve's interest rate decisions. The relationship between the Trump administration's economic policies and the Federal Reserve's rate outlook is being closely watched. Four different scenarios have been proposed to analyze this relationship and its potential effects on the economy. Powell's remarks highlight the challenges in predicting the future course of interest rates amid the current political climate.

news.headline: Core CPI inflation dropped compared to a year earlier, boosting hopes for Fed rate cuts. S&P 500 rallied on the news.
news.summary: In December, core inflation compared to the previous year surprisingly decreased. The Core Consumer Price Index (CPI) shows a decline, leading to a rise in the S&P 500 index as hopes for a Federal Reserve rate cut gain momentum.

news.headline: Next Fed meeting unlikely to have rate cut. When will next cut come and how will it affect S&P 500?
news.summary: The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting is not anticipated to result in a fourth consecutive rate reduction. Investors are curious about when the next rate decrease will occur and its significance for the S&P 500. This development is crucial as it impacts the stock market's performance and investor sentiment. Stay tuned for updates on future rate cuts and their potential implications for market dynamics.

news.headline: Fed halts rate cuts due to inflation, strong job market, and policy uncertainty. S&P 500 declines.
news.summary: Amid concerns of continued high inflation, a strong labor market, and uncertainty surrounding government policies, the Federal Reserve decided to halt further rate cuts. This decision came as President Trump introduced new policies, leading to a slight decrease in the S&P 500 index.

news.headline: Q4 GDP growth slowed, Fed's inflation gauge stable; S&P 500 futures rose post news.
news.summary: Economic growth in the fourth quarter experienced a slight slowdown, with the Gross Domestic Product seeing a moderate decrease. Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's inflation measure remained consistent with recent positive trends. S&P 500 futures showed an increase following these developments.

news.headline: Fed inflation still elevated, wages stable. S&P 500 eyes record high. Tariffs concerns ease.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure remains high, however, it has maintained positive readings for the past two months. S&P 500 futures experienced an increase in value. This positive trend in key inflation and wage data has alleviated concerns prior to the implementation of tariffs. The S&P 500 is now poised to potentially reach a record high.

news.headline: S&P 500 falls on Trump's high-risk tariff plan, launch date delayed. China tariffs unaffected.
news.summary: Following a decline, the S&P 500 rebounded as President Trump postponed tariffs on Mexico. This move showcased the ambitious nature of his strategies. The market responded with caution to his high-risk plan. Despite this development, tariffs on China remain in place. The delay in launching the tariffs was significant, impacting market trends and investor confidence.

news.headline: Hawkish jobs report and inflation expectations reduce chances of Fed rate cut; S&P 500 falls.
news.summary: An increase in inflation expectations and a relatively more hawkish jobs report have reduced the likelihood of a rate cut by the Fed. This news caused the S&P 500 to decline. The report highlighted concerns as the chance of a rate cut by the Fed decreased due to these factors. The data indicates a shift in the market sentiment following the release of the report. The job data showed a positive trend in employment, while inflation expectations rose, impacting the possibility of a future rate cut. Consequently, the S&P 500 experienced a drop, reflecting the market's reaction to this news.
